books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.noteText: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Souls of Black Folk
books.author: W.E.B. Dubois
books.rationale: This landmark text of American literature and sociology is paired with Up from Slavery by Booker T. Washington in the lesson plans. This pairing immerses students in the dialogues around the turn of the century as they read about Washington's life and aspirations and then consider Dubois's response.
books.noteText: Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Up from Slavery
books.author: Booker T. Washington
books.rationale: This is a first-person account of what it was like to be born on a slave plantation, live through the Civil War and Reconstruction, and make a new life as a free person. Washington's accomplishments and sense of hope are inspiring, and his founding of the Tuskegee Institute which continues as a historically black university and National Historic Site today is a remarkable achievement. Lesson plans pair Dubois's response to Washington to allow students a richer experience of the discussions of the times.
books.noteText:
Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.

Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas, translated and edited by Lowell Bair
books.rationale: A sweeping tale of (in)justice and revenge inspired by a real-life case, this historical adventure novel became one of Alexandre Dumas's most popular works.
books.noteText: Many different editions of this book exist, so if buying used be sure you are purchasing the Bantam Classics edition translated and abridged by Lowell Bair. Bair's version shortens the original work to a (somewhat) more manageable length without truncating the richness of the language and plot. If interested in reading the full text, we recommend Robin Buss's translation published by Penguin.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë, edited by Karen Swallow Prior
books.rationale: A coming of age story narrated in first person and set in 19th century England. Students experience the world through Jane's eyes and wrestle along with her when she faces various events and quandaries.
books.noteText:
This edition includes the full text along with reflection questions and an introduction. Since the lesson plans refer to this additional content, students should use this edition if possible.  Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose Jane Eyre OR James. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

books.title: James
books.author: Percival Everett
books.rationale: Recently published and already widely acclaimed, James retells Twain's Adventures of Huckleberry Finn from Jim's point of view. Funny and intense by turns and always insightful, Everett immerses students in 19th-century America. Alveary lessons pair selections of Huckleberry Finn alongside James to enrich students' understanding of the texts and times.
books.noteText:
Only recommended for mature 11-12th grade students. Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose James OR Jane Eyre. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

books.title: Short stories and other writings
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate a selection of writings from a variety of 19th century authors such as Pushkin, O.Henry, Tagore, and others to give students exposure to a broader range of genres, authors, and ideas.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.author: Chinua Achebe
books.rationale: Widely considered to be the foundational text of modern African fiction, Things Fall Apart provides a detailed, unflinching depiction of the complex life and beliefs of the Igbo tribe of Nigeria in the late 1800s, before and after their first encounters with European missionaries and the establishment of a colonial government. The story traces the life of the warrior Okonkwo: his upbringing and adulthood, his rise to prominence and and tragic fall, and finally his banishment and return to find his home forever changed. Written in English from an authentically African perspective, Achebe's novel directly challenges Western narratives of colonialism and stereotypes of Africa, yet eschews any simple resolutions.
books.noteText: Recommended for mature Grades 11-12 students due to sensitive subject matter. Students who might need a lighter read may choose a title from the Grades 9-10 literature selections.
